signals.title: AUD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7047 — USD outperformance (+0.692%) vs AUD underperformance (-0.734%) creates a 1.426% G10 spread, the widest bilateral theme in the complex. The divergence is fundamentally supported by the Economic Surprise Index (CESI), where USD net +29 beats contrast with AUD net -8 misses. Despite a 60bp nominal carry advantage for AUD (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), the bear steepener regime (2s10s +92.7bp) and blowout US nonfarm payroll headlines favor the USD. Watch for a break of 0.7000 to confirm trend extension.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.105 — Price holds above the 160.00 psychological level, a zone previously triggering BoJ verbal warnings and intervention. While the +289bp OIS carry (SOFR 3.62% vs TONA 0.73%) sustains the bid, the options market is pricing downside protection with a 25d RR of -1.14 (moderate put skew). COT LF net short -105,136 remains extreme, suggesting a crowded carry trade vulnerable to BoJ policy shifts at the June 16 meeting. Sustained trading above 160.00 increases the probability of direct MoF intervention.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail vs Institutional Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5813 — Retail sentiment is 88% long, directly fading the institutional trend where COT LF net short is -27,797 (15.1% long). This extreme retail long positioning at the bottom of a 1M -2.37% move signals high capitulation risk if price breaks recent lows. The real rate differential favors USD (+1.23%) over NZD (-0.83%), providing a structural headwind for the pair. Watch for retail long liquidation to accelerate the move toward 0.5750.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + ECB Event Risk
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1541 — Options market is pricing an elevated +3.0% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.1%) ahead of the June 11 ECB meeting. Despite a +169bp OIS carry advantage for USD, EUR/USD intraday is +0.12% as markets assess a potential ECB hike bias. COT LF net short -22,320 indicates specs are positioned for further downside, but the high VRP suggests the market is paying up for protection against a hawkish ECB surprise. Watch the 1.1500 level for support ahead of the policy announcement.

signals.title: DXY vs SPX — Correlation Break + Funding Stress
signals.text: DXY vs SPX correlation at -0.456 vs 252d norm of -0.290 (z=+1.59σ) — The strengthening negative correlation during a period of USD outperformance (+0.692% 1W avg) signals a return to the 'USD as a safe-haven' regime. With the DXY testing 100.00 and US 10Y yields rising to 4.552%, the positive z-score indicates that equity strength is failing to dampen USD demand, likely due to unresolved U.S.-Iran geopolitical tensions and restrictive real rates (+1.23%).
